message:
test(readme): readme_apply_and_transform_compiles_and_runs (fd90.181 / frankenpandas-0kypg)

Implements the integration test registered in
frankenpandas-0kypg. Mirrors the README's "Apply and Transform"
section (lines 643-673) end-to-end so a regression in any of the
documented user-facing apply/transform/pipe/assign_fn surfaces
fails the test rather than breaking the docs silently. Locks in
the prior fix-ups: fd90.107 (transform closure / GroupBy string
variant), fd90.108 (apply_row name arg), fd90.134 (pipe
FrameError chain), and fd90.180 (assign_fn inline ratio
expression).

Coverage:

- DataFrame::applymap(|s: &Scalar| ...): element-wise closure on
  every cell. Test path doubles Int64 / Float64 values and
  passes other dtypes through unchanged, so the test exercises
  both the type-dispatched fast path and the identity fallback.

- DataFrame::apply_row("name", |row_values: &[Scalar]| ...):
  fd90.108 contract. Sums numeric values per row and returns
  Scalar::Float64; asserts the resulting Series has one entry
  per original row.

- DataFrame::transform(|s: &Scalar| ...): closure variant from
  fd90.107. Returns same-shape DataFrame.

- DataFrame::groupby(...).transform("mean"): string variant from
  fd90.107. Broadcasts per-group mean back to original rows;
  asserts result has one row per ORIGINAL row (not per group),
  which is the contract that distinguishes transform from agg.

- DataFrame::assign_fn(vec![("ratio", Box::new(|df| ->
  Result<Column, FrameError>))])?: fd90.180 inline expression.
  Computes a derived column by closure and appends it to the
  frame.

- DataFrame::pipe(|d| d.method())?.pipe(|d| d.method())?:
  fd90.134 chain. Verifies the FrameError type bubbles through
  pipe so callers can use ? at every link.

Pure additive test; uses prelude-only imports per the existing
file convention. No source/library changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
